channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: African leaders collaborate on border governance and migration challenges
item.description: King Misuzulu kaZwelithini has emphasized the need for enhanced cooperation among African nations to address illegal immigration and border governance during a high-level meeting with Zimbabwean Presi...

item.title: Windsor Judge Dismisses $13M Drug Smuggling Charges Over Charter Rights Violations
item.description: A Windsor judge has dismissed $13 million drug smuggling charges against cross-border truck driver Harinder Singh Saini, citing 'egregious' Charter rights violations by Canada Border Services Agency (...

item.title: Critique of Government Report on Antifeminist Ideologies and Gender Issues
item.description: The article critiques a Canadian government report titled 'Confronting Antifeminist Ideologies in Canada,' which focuses on online trends like 'looksmaxxing' (a term associated with the 'manosphere' a...

item.title: Vybz Kartel and Sidem Face Rumors of Split After New Woman Appears in Video
item.description: Vybz Kartel, the Jamaican dancehall star, has sparked rumors of a split with his long-time fiancée Sidem after posting a video of a casual outdoor gathering. The clip shows him at a relaxed event with...

item.title: Yanga delays Mwamnyeto contract renewal over financial terms
item.description: Young Africans (Yanga) are delaying the renewal of Bakari Mwamnyeto's contract due to financial considerations. The defender, who joined Yanga from Coastal Union in 2020, has become a key player in th...

item.title: Railway linen theft costs contractors ₹104 crore; 56% rise in thefts
item.description: An RTI-based investigation by The Indian Express reveals that between January 2022 and May 2026, at least 1.27 crore bedroll items were stolen from Indian Railways trains, causing a loss of ₹104.51 cr...

item.title: FIFA Removes French Referees from 2026 World Cup Roster Amid Speculation
item.description: FIFA has removed two prominent French referees, François Letexier and Clément Turpin, from the 2026 World Cup roster. While initial speculation linked their exclusion to a controversial match between ...

item.title: Tanzania Faces Human Rights Concerns Over President Samia's Remarks
item.description: Tanzanian President Samia Suluhu Hassan faces criticism from human rights groups and opposition parties over recent statements that are perceived as undermining civil liberties. The controversy stems ...

item.title: Two Shabu Dealers Arrested in Maguindanao del Norte with Family Assistance
item.description: Two individuals involved in selling shabu near school areas were arrested in Maguindanao del Norte after their relatives and local leaders facilitated the operation. The suspects, Bairuth Paches and A...

item.title: India imposes ban on imports linked to forced labor amid US trade probe
item.description: India has announced a ban on the import of goods produced using forced labor, a move aimed at avoiding potential US tariffs. The decision, made by the trade ministry, empowers the central government t...

item.title: Kenyans Curious About Sheryl Gabriella's Alleged Mansion and Wealth
item.description: A video of Kenyan TikToker Sheryl Gabriella giving a room-by-room tour of what she claims is her multimillion-shilling mansion has gone viral, sparking debates about her financial status. The clip, sh...

item.title: The Enduring Legacy of Gavin & Stacey: A Love Story That Captivated Britain
item.description: Gavin & Stacey, created by Ruth Jones and James Corden, became one of Britain’s most beloved TV couples through its authentic portrayal of love and family. The show, which aired from 2007 to 2010, fol...

item.title: Deputy US Marshal Killed During Arrest Warrant Execution in Louisiana
item.description: A Deputy U.S. Marshal was tragically killed on Monday while serving an arrest warrant in Alexandria, Louisiana. The incident occurred around 3 p.m. in the Rutland Road area when authorities were attem...

item.title: Businessman Faces Court Over Alleged Property Damage to Rival Rehab Centre
item.description: Prominent businessman and rehab centre owner Phillimon Moloto, known as Mr Moloto, is appearing in Polokwane magistrate’s court accused of malicious damage to property. The case involves allegations t...

item.title: ANC Struggles with Mayoral Candidate Selection Ahead of South Africa's Municipal Elections
item.description: The African National Congress (ANC), South Africa's dominant political party, is grappling with the challenge of selecting mayoral candidates ahead of the November municipal elections. This marks the ...

item.title: Nigerian Deaf Lawyer Nehemiah Danjuma Becomes First From North To Be Called To Bar
item.description: Nehemiah Danjuma has broken barriers by becoming Nigeria's first deaf lawyer from the north after being admitted to the Nigerian Bar. The achievement marks seven years of resilience, with Danjuma over...

item.title: Pakistan and Turkey Strengthen Defense Cooperation and Regional Security Ties
item.description: Chief of Defence Forces (CDF) Syed Asim Munir met Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan in Ankara to discuss regional security, defense cooperation, and strategic partnerships between Pakistan and Tu...

item.title: Parents of Agniveer seek pension after son's death in Operation Sindoor
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing struggle of Jyothibai Naik and her husband Shriramnaik, parents of Agniveer Mood Muralinaik, who died in Operation Sindoor. Despite the government honoring their son ...

item.title: E Atkinson to miss second Test against New Zealand after curfew breach and ECB probe into nightclub incident involving Saracens star
item.description: England's cricket team has faced a setback as fast bowler E Atkinson is set to miss the second Test against New Zealand following allegations of breaching team curfew rules. The England and Wales Cric...

item.title: NYSC urges corps members to boost income through entrepreneurship
item.description: The Director-General of the National Youth Service Corps (NYSC), Brigadier General Olakunle Nafiu, has advised corps members in Kogi State to prioritize entrepreneurship over relying solely on monthly...
